message:
chore(beads): sync issues.jsonl with ProvisioningRecipe pin transition

Single-line drift sync absorbing the open → in_progress transition for
bead flywheel_connectors-pbfjt ("ProvisioningRecipe Display + serde"),
which the parallel agent flipped while implementing the feature +
pinning test that landed in f5f659114. The bead will move to closed
on the next agent sync; this commit just keeps the JSONL consistent
with the workflow events emitted between syncs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2312,7 +2312,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-p5ams","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X: HelloReplayWindow state-machine fuzz (FIFO eviction + (from,nonce) keying)","description":"fcp_protocol::session::HelloReplayWindow (session.rs:553-599) is the
bounded FIFO replay-protection window for handshake hellos. The
`verify_hello_attested_with_replay` path (session.rs:650-662) gates new
sessions on `check_and_update` returning true; a regression in the
window's bookkeeping would either:
  - admit a duplicate (from, nonce) that's still within the active
    window → handshake-replay attack surface re-opened.
  - evict and re-admit prematurely → still a replay window leak.
  - cap-bypass (seen.len() exceeds capacity) → DoS amplification surface
    if attackers spray distinct (from, nonce) tuples.

Existing fcp-protocol fuzz coverage in this area:
  - session_metamorphic        — verify_session_mac binding MRs
  - session_cookie_binding     — verify_cookie field-binding (674lv)
  - derive_session_keys        — KDF binding MRs
  - session_transcript / session — handshake decode

NOT covered: HelloReplayWindow's state-machine invariants.

Properties asserted (state-machine MRs):

  1. **First-time accept**: a hello not in the window MUST be accepted
     by check_and_update.
  2. **Immediate-replay reject**: re-inserting the SAME hello MUST be
     rejected; check returns false in lockstep.
  3. **check ⇔ check_and_update agreement**: if check returns false,
     check_and_update MUST also return false; if check returns true,
     check_and_update MUST return true on the first call.
  4. **FIFO eviction with re-admit**: after capacity+N unique inserts
     (N ≥ 1), the OLDEST key MUST have been evicted, and a re-insert
     of that key MUST be accepted again (was outside the window).
  5. **Capacity bound enforcement**: at all times seen.len() and
     order.len() are ≤ capacity.
  6. **(from, nonce) keying**: two hellos differing in `from` only
     MUST be tracked independently (both accepted on first insert);
     two hellos differing in `nonce` only MUST also be tracked
     independently. A regression that hashed only one field would
     surface here.

Note: HelloReplayWindow.seen / .order are private; we observe state
indirectly via check + check_and_update return values, which is how
the security boundary is actually exposed to callers.

Acceptance:
  - fuzz/fuzz_targets/hello_replay_window.rs
  - 6 properties + once-gated regression anchors for (a) FIFO
    eviction at capacity boundary and (b) (from, nonce) keying.
  - Registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml.
  - cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:14:18.986114Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T22:15:43.208793Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T22:15:43.208330Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3084,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p5ams","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped in commit ecb82520. fuzz/fuzz_targets/hello_replay_window.rs — 4 properties driven by an external VecDeque FIFO model (first-time accept, immediate-replay reject, check⇔check_and_update agreement against the model on every op, (from,nonce) keying parity) + once-gated anchors for capacity-boundary FIFO eviction (h0..h3 with capacity=3) and same-nonce-different-from / same-from-different-nonce keying. cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:15:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-p6c11","title":"[REVIEW-A9-LINT][P3] connectors/firecrawl/src/client.rs:33: undocumented #[allow]","description":"Production-code #[allow(clippy::missing_errors_doc, clippy::unused_async)] at connectors/firecrawl/src/client.rs:33 has no brief justification comment immediately above it. Either remove the suppression or add a concise comment explaining why the lint is intentionally suppressed.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T03:46:03.956266Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T04:25:30.578005Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T04:25:30.577571Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 2175bc7a: added justification comment for firecrawl client allow","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","title":"[MOR/C3.5] Change default simulate() to deny; fix default-deny violation","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:57.765589Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T22:36:36.489235Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T21:51:37.610429Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["P0-critical","default-deny","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8whb3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:07.503486Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2644,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Change the default implementation of simulate() in the FcpConnector trait from returning SimulateResponse::allowed(req.id) to returning SimulateResponse::denied(req.id, reason: operation_not_simulated). WHY: J1 Deontic found this is a DIRECT VIOLATION of the default-deny principle. A connector that does not override simulate() currently implicitly claims ALL operations are allowed. This means a new connector scaffold that ships without implementing simulate() silently tells the system it can handle anything. The correct default is: deny everything the connector has not explicitly declared it can simulate. WHERE: crates/fcp-core/src/connector.rs or crates/fcp-sdk/src/lib.rs — the default impl of simulate() in the FcpConnector trait. Also update fwc new_cmd.rs scaffold template to include a simulate() override with proper operation matching. ACCEPTANCE: (1) Default simulate() returns denied. (2) Existing connectors that rely on default must be updated (or explicitly override with allowed for their operations). (3) fwc new scaffold includes simulate() override template. (4) At least 3 tests: default returns denied, override returns allowed, denied includes reason code. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 1 day for the default change + 0.5 day per connector that needs updating. Quick audit needed: how many connectors rely on the default simulate()? DESIGN NOTE: This is a one-line change with outsized security impact. It converts simulate() from opt-out safety (dangerous default) to opt-in safety (safe default). Any connector that was relying on the old default was ALREADY WRONG — it was claiming support for operations it had not validated.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:36:50Z"},{"id":2652,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verified complete: default simulate() returns SimulateResponse::denied(FCP-3010) since connector.rs:81-89. Scaffold template in new_cmd.rs:2126 has full simulate() override with operation validation, state checks, and capability verification. protocol.rs has 3+ SimulateResponse tests (allowed, denied with reason/code, with_cost). 44 connectors override simulate() explicitly; remaining ~46 correctly use default-deny. All acceptance criteria met.","created_at":"2026-04-10T21:51:16Z"},{"id":2653,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Changed default simulate() from allowed→denied (FCP-3010). Fixed pre-existing phantom type compile errors. 4 new tests, 3898 lib tests pass, clippy clean. Commit 0ce30890.","created_at":"2026-04-10T22:02:45Z"},{"id":2657,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-p8clj","author":"jemanuel","text":"C3.5 Default simulate() to deny — already implemented. FcpConnector::simulate() returns SimulateResponse::denied('default deny — override simulate() to provide dry-run capability') as verified in connector.rs. No changes needed.","created_at":"2026-04-10T22:36:36Z"}]}

+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-pbfjt","title":"Pin fcp-core ProvisioningRecipe Display + serde","description":"Add tests in crates/fcp-core/tests/ pinning ProvisioningRecipe Display formatting and serde JSON+CBOR roundtrip.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"cod5","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:40:02.307833Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:46:50.510825Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pcmm8","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] fcp-registry supply-chain config enforcement is trivially bypassable","description":"crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:1679-1708 (commit 0f14b9c9 enforce supply-chain verification config) — enforce_supply_chain_verification_config treats TUF required and Sigstore required as satisfied whenever ANY SupplyChainEvidence is passed (even a default one with transparency_log_present=false and empty attestations). SupplyChainEvidence has no tuf_verified or sigstore_verified fields (crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:290), so the config-level require_tuf/require_sigstore guards are presence-only and give operators a false sense of enforcement. Suggested fix: add verified flags to SupplyChainEvidence, populated ONLY by real verifier adapters; require those bools to be true when required. Until then, consider failing these branches with a runtime Internal error until real verification wires up, so operators do not deploy with require_tuf=true thinking they are protected. Reviewer: cc_1.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:02:16.384406Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:38:48.353936Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:38:48.352678Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2826,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pcmm8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in commit c046efde.

**Fix summary**:
- SupplyChainEvidence gains tuf_verified + sigstore_verified booleans (populated only by real verifier adapters).
- enforce_supply_chain_verification_config now requires those bools to be true, not merely evidence.is_some().
- fwc production sites (build_registry_supply_chain_evidence in supply_chain_cmd.rs, RegistrySupplyChainEvidence in main.rs) carry both at false with explicit SECURITY comments — those paths only validate publisher signatures, so deployments with require_tuf/require_sigstore will now correctly fail-closed there until real adapter wiring lands (matching the reviewer's intent).
- 34 struct-literal construction sites patched (compile-driven).

**Regression tests** added to the ::tests module:
- verify_bundle_rejects_unverified_tuf_evidence — asserts unverified evidence rejected + positive control (tuf_verified=true satisfies gate)
- verify_bundle_rejects_unverified_sigstore_evidence — same for sigstore

**Verification**:
- cargo check -p fcp-registry -p fwc --all-targets: clean
- cargo test -p fcp-registry --lib: 354 pass (was 352 + 2 new), 6 pre-existing fail (mock_registry_*, metamorphic_install_verify — ObjectStore::AlreadyExists + manifest_signing_bytes unrelated)
- cargo test -p fcp-registry --features test-mocks --test no_mock_integration: 49 pass, 39 pre-existing fail (unrelated manifest validation rule for operation capability declaration). Confirmed baseline-equivalent via stash-and-rerun.

— cod_4 / SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:38:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pd6cv","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X: SessionCookie::try_from_slice length gate + decode_cookie_bytes agreement","description":"Fuzz fcp_protocol::SessionCookie::try_from_slice (session.rs:240-247) + decode_cookie_bytes (session.rs:507-509). Both gate on slice.len()==SESSION_COOKIE_SIZE (32). Properties: (1) length-32 slice → Ok with bytes preserved verbatim; (2) length-not-32 slice → Err(InvalidCookieLength{len: input.len()}) carrying exact length; (3) decode_cookie_bytes agrees with try_from_slice byte-for-byte; (4) as_bytes round-trip identity; (5) bytes32_serde JSON round-trip; (6) bytes32_serde CBOR round-trip.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T00:47:14.278916Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T00:48:21.206464Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T00:48:21.205957Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3200,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pd6cv","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped fuzz_session_cookie_try_from_slice (commit 37f7fece). Covers SessionCookie::try_from_slice (session.rs:240) and decode_cookie_bytes (session.rs:507). Properties: length-32 acceptance, length-not-32 → InvalidCookieLength{len:input.len()}, wrapper agreement, round-trip identity, JSON+CBOR serde round-trips. Anchors hit boundary lengths 0/1/31/32/33/64.","created_at":"2026-04-28T00:48:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pdgc","title":"TEST: fcp-config Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-config Unit Test Suite

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for configuration validation and schema framework.

## Test Categories

### Schema Validation
- [ ] JSON Schema validation
- [ ] Required field enforcement
- [ ] Type validation
- [ ] Format validation (URL, email, etc.)
- [ ] Custom validators

### Configuration Loading
- [ ] TOML parsing
- [ ] JSON parsing
- [ ] Environment variable expansion
- [ ] File path resolution
- [ ] Default value handling

### Secret Reference Resolution
- [ ] Secret placeholder parsing
- [ ] Vault integration mocking
- [ ] Environment variable secrets
- [ ] File-based secrets

### Validation Errors
- [ ] Clear error messages
- [ ] Path to invalid field
- [ ] Suggested fixes
- [ ] Multiple error collection

### Configuration Merging
- [ ] Override precedence
- [ ] Deep merge behavior
- [ ] Array handling

## Coverage Target: >95%","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T17:12:48.109383666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:34.771665015Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T08:22:34.771665015Z","close_reason":"Superseded - parent bead fcp-config is closed. Config testing is now part of manifest testing (1n78.27.1).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pdgc","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-5y7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
